howItWorks.label: How It Works
howItWorks.title: From Signal to Sovereignty
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps to Sybil-resistant verification. No hardware. No biometrics. Pure physics.

0.title: Initiate Your Presence Commitment
0.description: Your device submits a cryptographic commitment — a hash of your presence claim. No passwords, no biometrics, no dedicated hardware. One tap to enter the verification epoch.
0.iconAlt: Phone connecting to the network with signal arcs

1.title: Witness Circles Triangulate Your Signal
1.description: Validators form witness circles, measuring network latency from multiple points to triangulate your position — no GPS, no oracles, just physics.

2.title: Proof Finalized On‑Chain
2.description: Once quorum is reached, your presence proof is sealed — immutable, sovereign, and impossible to forge or replay across epochs.
2.iconAlt: Hexagonal seal with checkmark chained to blocks

whyChain.title: Why
whyChain.subtitle: Every other chain trusts cryptography alone. 7aychain anchors identity to physical reality — the one primitive AI, bots, and Sybil farms cannot forge.

0.title: Physics Over Biometrics
0.description: No iris scans. No fingerprints. Validators measure network latency and signal timing to verify you’re physically present — physics that bots can’t fake.

1.title: Commit‑Reveal Privacy Architecture
1.description: Your secret never touches the chain. Commit a hash, reveal later. Nullifiers prevent replay across epochs. ZK proofs let you prove presence without exposing identity.

2.title: Autonomous Sybil Scoring
2.description: The autonomous pallet scores every actor 0–100 on behavioral patterns. No CAPTCHAs, no orbs — the protocol separates humans from machines automatically.

3.title: Presence‑Weighted Governance
3.description: Capability-based permissions, not token voting. Delegations cascade up to 5 levels deep. Revoke a root capability and every child revokes with it — all on-chain.

description.0: 7aychain verifies that real humans are in real places — using the physics of internet signals, not cameras, not biometrics, not special hardware.
description.1: A blockchain built for the one thing AI cannot fake: being somewhere.

techHowItWorks.label: How It Works
techHowItWorks.title: The Principles Behind the
techHowItWorks.subtitle: Four foundations that make presence verification possible.

0.title: Rooted in Physics
0.description: Internet signals travel at known speeds. By measuring timing from multiple validators, the network estimates your physical location — no GPS, no oracles, just the laws of light.

1.title: Private by Default
1.description: The network proves you were present without ever seeing where. Your location data is committed as a hash, verified through ZK proofs, and never stored in plaintext on-chain.

2.title: Economically Secured
2.description: Every validator stakes real value. Lie about someone’s location and you lose your stake. Honesty is the only profitable strategy.

3.title: Unforgeable by AI
3.description: AI can generate text, images, and synthetic identities. But it cannot physically exist at a coordinate. Presence is the proof of humanity that resists automation.

presenceJourney.label: Protocol Flow
presenceJourney.title: From Signal to
presenceJourney.subtitle: Three steps. No middlemen. Your presence, verified and permanent.

0.title: You Show Up
0.description: Your device submits a cryptographic commitment — a sealed hash that says “I am here” without revealing where. One action. No biometrics. No hardware.

1.title: The Network Measures You
1.description: A circle of validators independently measure signal timing from multiple points. Through multilateration they estimate your position — without you sharing it.

2.title: Your Proof Is Sealed
2.description: When enough validators agree, your presence is finalized on-chain — an immutable record that you were physically present. You own it. No one can revoke it.

architectureLayers.label: Architecture
architectureLayers.title: Four Layers,
architectureLayers.subtitle: Each layer builds on the one below it, from raw networking to the applications you interact with.

0.title: Network Foundation
0.description: How nodes find each other, agree on truth, and produce blocks. Consensus ensures every validator plays by the same rules.

1.title: Presence Engine
1.description: The core innovation: measuring network latency from multiple points to estimate physical position. Witness circles form, measure, and triangulate — all without GPS.

2.title: Security & Privacy
2.description: Multiple layers of protection: validators stake value, disputes are resolved on-chain, ZK proofs keep your location private.

3.title: Applications
3.description: What gets built on verified presence: governance weighted by participation, secure vaults, trust networks, and coordination tools.

description.0: Zero-knowledge proofs let the network confirm you were at a location without learning where that location is. Your data stays with you.
description.1: The protocol uses cryptographic commitments so that even the validators who measure your signal never see your actual coordinates. They only confirm: “yes, this person was physically present.”

0.title: Commit-Reveal
0.description: Submit a sealed hash of your presence first. Only after it’s locked do you reveal the data — preventing tampering.

1.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs
1.description: Mathematical proofs that verify “this person was here” without revealing where. The network never sees your location.

2.title: Nullifier Protection
2.description: Each proof generates a unique nullifier preventing reuse — stopping replay attacks while preserving anonymity.

useCasesPage.label: Use Cases
useCasesPage.title: Where Presence Changes Everything
useCasesPage.description: From fair voting to verified reviews — real-world problems that only physical presence can solve.

sybilProblem.title: The Sybil problem is everywhere
sybilProblem.paragraph1: The same person can create a thousand fake accounts in minutes. Bots farm rewards. Fake users capture votes. And most systems have no way to tell the difference.
sybilProblem.paragraph2: The root cause is simple: there's no reliable way to prove a unique human is behind an action.

physicsSolution.title: Physics as the solution
physicsSolution.paragraph1: 7aychain solves this with physics. You can create unlimited accounts — but you can only be in one place at a time. By measuring internet connection timing from multiple points, the network confirms you're real and present.
physicsSolution.paragraph2: No eye scans. No special devices.

useCasesPage.applicationsSubtitle: Real-world problems that only physical presence can solve.

0.title: Uniquely Human
0.description: Every verification is grounded in the physics of your real-world presence.

1.title: Private by Default
1.description: Verifies you're present without knowing who you are or tracking you.

2.title: Permanently Recorded
2.description: Every proof is stored on a public record that anyone can verify.

3.title: Works Everywhere
3.description: Any device with internet. No special equipment required.

0.title: Fair Token Drops
0.description: Rewards go to real people, not bots farming thousands of accounts. Every recipient is verified as a unique human who was actually present.
0.detail: Token distributions become meaningful when every recipient is a real, verified person. No more drained budgets from fake accounts and automated farming.

1.title: Fair Voting
1.description: One person, one vote — guaranteed by physics. No wallet farms, no vote buying, no governance capture by fake accounts.
1.detail: Communities can run votes, proposals, and elections knowing that each participant is a real person — not a collection of wallets controlled by one actor.

2.title: Secure Transactions
2.description: Add a physical security layer to high-value operations. Even if someone steals your password, they'd need to be physically present to act.
2.detail: Critical financial operations gain a layer of protection that no hacker can bypass remotely — they would need to physically be at the right place.

3.title: Event Ticketing
3.description: Prove you attended a conference, meetup, or workshop. Earn rewards for actually being there — not just claiming you were.
3.detail: Event organizers get accurate attendance data while attendees earn verifiable credentials that carry real weight.

4.title: Verified Communities
4.description: Build communities around people who actually show up. No fake members, no bots padding the numbers.
4.detail: Membership and access are earned through real participation, not manufactured accounts or purchased credentials.

5.title: Verified Reviews & Deliveries
5.description: Only people who actually visited can leave reviews. Drivers prove they arrived at the right address. End fake reviews and delivery fraud.
5.detail: Physical verification ensures that reviews, deliveries, and service confirmations come from people who were actually there.

glossaryPage.label: Glossary
glossaryPage.title: 7aychain Terminology
glossaryPage.description: Definitions of key terms, pallets, and concepts in the 7aychain Proof of Presence protocol.

autonomousDetection.definition: An on-chain module that automatically detects anomalous patterns in presence attestations, flagging suspicious behavior for review or dispute without requiring manual reports.

boomerangProof.definition: A cryptographic mechanism that verifies an actor returned to a previously attested location. Used to prove consistent presence patterns over time without revealing the location itself.

circuitRegistry.definition: The on-chain registry of ZK circuits available for proof generation and verification. Manages circuit versions, migration between proof modes, and verifier configurations.

commitRevealScheme.definition: A two-phase protocol where actors first commit a hash of their presence data (hiding it), then reveal the actual data in a later phase. Prevents front-running and ensures fair ordering of presence declarations.

deviceScanner.definition: A pallet-level module for registering and scanning devices that participate in presence attestation. Handles device identity, attestation capability, and revocation.

disputeResolution.definition: An on-chain process for challenging presence attestations. Disputants submit evidence, validators vote, and the protocol enforces outcomes including slashing for dishonest attestors.

epoch.definition: A fixed time window during which presence declarations, attestations, and finalizations occur. Epochs structure the protocol lifecycle and determine validator rotation schedules.

groth16.definition: A zero-knowledge proof system used by 7aychain for succinct, non-interactive presence proofs. Provides constant-size proofs with fast verification, enabling efficient on-chain ZK verification.

laudNetworksCli.definition: A Python-based terminal user interface (TUI) for interacting with 7aychain pallets. Supports all 16 protocol modules for presence declaration, validator management, ZK proofs, and more.

lifecycle.definition: The full state machine governing a presence declaration from creation to finalization: Declare, Commit, Reveal, Attest, Triangulate, and Finalize.

multilateration.definition: A positioning technique that estimates an actor's physical location using network latency measurements from multiple validator reference points. The core triangulation algorithm in witness circles.

octopusGroups.definition: Organizational units within the protocol that group validators and actors for coordinated presence operations. Groups can have shared governance rules and collective attestation thresholds.

positionBoundToken.definition: A cryptographic token minted when presence is finalized. PBTs tie attestation claims to specific positions and epochs, serving as verifiable proof-of-presence credentials without revealing exact locations.

presenceLifecycle.definition: The six-phase protocol flow: Declare (announce intent), Commit (submit presence hash), Reveal (disclose data), Attest (validators measure), Triangulate (compute position), Finalize (quorum consensus).

proofOfPresence.definition: The consensus mechanism of 7aychain. Validators form witness circles and triangulate physical presence through network latency measurement -- no GPS, no biometrics, no external hardware.

quorumConsensus.definition: The threshold of validator agreement required to finalize a presence attestation. A presence declaration is only finalized when a sufficient percentage of the witness circle converges on a position estimate.

semanticLinks.definition: On-chain relationships between entities (actors, locations, organizations) that form a trust graph. Semantic links enable contextual verification and reputation based on presence history.

shamirSecretSharing.definition: A cryptographic scheme used in the vault system to split sensitive data across multiple parties. No single party holds enough information to reconstruct the secret, enabling secure recovery and sharing.

snark.definition: Succinct Non-interactive Argument of Knowledge. A zero-knowledge proof type used by 7aychain for compact, efficiently verifiable presence proofs. The protocol migrates from stub verifiers to full SNARK circuits.

storageLayer.definition: A pallet providing on-chain and off-chain data storage for presence proofs, attestation records, and protocol state. Supports pinning, retrieval, and data availability guarantees.

triangulation.definition: The process by which validators estimate an actor's physical position using network latency measurements from multiple reference points. Combines multilateration with centroid algorithms for position estimation.

validator.definition: A node operator who stakes $7AY to participate in consensus, presence attestation, and witness circle triangulation. Validators earn rewards for honest behavior and face slashing for dishonesty.

vault.definition: A secure on-chain storage system for sensitive data. Supports Shamir secret sharing for distributed key management, enabling data recovery and controlled sharing between authorized parties.

witnessCircle.definition: A pseudorandomly selected subset of validators assigned to attest a presence declaration. Circle members independently measure latency and collectively triangulate the declaring actor's position.

zkCircuit.definition: A zero-knowledge computation circuit that generates proofs verifying presence without revealing exact location data. 7aychain uses Groth16 circuits with an on-chain registry and migration path from stub to full SNARK verification.

whyPresencePage.label: Why Presence
whyPresencePage.title: The Internet Lost the Human Signal
whyPresencePage.description: When bots can do everything humans can do online, the only thing left that proves you're real is being physically present.

presenceInfrastructure.paragraph1: At the heart of 7aychain is a simple idea: your internet connection can prove where you are. Validators across the network measure connection timing from multiple points, confirming your physical location without GPS, cameras, or special hardware.
presenceInfrastructure.paragraph2: Most systems try to figure out if you're real by looking at your behavior, your accounts, or your credentials. At scale, all of those can be faked. 7aychain takes a different approach: it uses the laws of physics.

physicsArgument.label: The Physics Argument
physicsArgument.title: Sybil resistance through physical reality
physicsArgument.subtitle: Instead of trusting profiles, credentials, or biometrics, 7aychain exploits a fundamental physical constraint: you can only be in one place at a time.

0.title: Physics, Not Scans
0.description: Internet signals have a speed limit — the speed of light. 7aychain uses this fact to confirm where you are. No iris scans, no selfies, no fingerprints.

1.title: One Place at a Time
1.description: You can create a million accounts, but you can only physically be in one spot. That's the constraint that makes faking presence nearly impossible.

2.title: No Lasting Footprint
2.description: Each verification is a one-time event. No persistent profile, no behavioral tracking, no data stored about you. Prove you were here, then it's done.

coreInsight.label: Core Insight
coreInsight.title: In a world of perfect simulation, physical reality becomes the scarce resource
coreInsight.description: AI can generate convincing text, behavior, and interaction at scale. But replicating physical presence — being at a specific location at a specific time, measurable through network physics — remains constrained by the laws of nature.

0.problem: Bots can fake behavior at scale
0.response: AI can fake text, transactions, and social activity. But it can't fake being somewhere — physical presence is constrained by the laws of physics.

1.response: Making a new wallet costs nothing. Being in two places at once is impossible. 7aychain turns a digital problem into a physical one.

2.response: Iris scans and facial recognition create permanent databases of your body. 7aychain measures internet timing — it never sees your face or knows your name.

3.response: Coordinated groups can create fake social proof. Physical presence is independently verified by the network — no trust required.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.title: How 7aychain compares
comparison.subtitle: Different approaches to the human verification problem. Each makes different trade-offs on privacy, hardware, and what they actually prove.

0.worldcoin: Scans your iris with a special device
0.brightid: Friends vouch for you on video
0.passport: Links your online accounts for a trust score

3.brightid: Medium — your face is seen on video

4.sevenychain: You're physically at a real location

multiNodeSetup.label: Multi-Node Setup
multiNodeSetup.title: 6-Node Network Setup
multiNodeSetup.description: Run a full 6-validator devnet locally. Alice runs as the native boot node while the remaining validators can run via Docker in hybrid mode.

dockerHybrid.title: Docker hybrid mode
dockerHybrid.code:
# Start Alice natively
./target/release/seveny-node \
  --chain local \
  --alice \
  --port 30333 \
  --rpc-port 9944 \
  --validator

# Start remaining nodes via Docker
docker-compose up bob charlie dave eve ferdie

validatorsPage.label: Validators
validatorsPage.title: Run a 7aychain Validator
validatorsPage.description: Validators power the 7aychain network. They form groups, measure internet connections, confirm people's locations, and keep the network honest.

roleOverview.label: Role Overview
roleOverview.title: What validators do
roleOverview.paragraph1: On 7aychain, validators do more than keep the network running. They actively verify that people are where they claim to be — forming groups that independently confirm each person's location using internet connection measurements.
roleOverview.paragraph2: Each validator in a witness circle measures network latency to the declaring actor and to other validators. These measurements feed into multilateration algorithms that triangulate physical position — without GPS or external hardware.
roleOverview.paragraph3: When enough validators agree on someone's location, their presence is confirmed and permanently recorded. Honest validators earn rewards. Dishonest ones lose their stake.
roleOverview.paragraph4: The validator set rotates per epoch, ensuring no single group of validators can collude to falsify presence attestations over time.

prerequisites.label: Prerequisites
prerequisites.title: Install dependencies
prerequisites.description: 7aychain requires Rust (stable), the WASM compilation target, Clang/LLVM, and protobuf. Choose your operating system below.

0.commands:
brew install cmake pkg-config openssl git llvm protobuf
rustup default stable
rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown

1.commands:
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y \
  cmake pkg-config libssl-dev git clang \
  libclang-dev protobuf-compiler
rustup default stable
rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown

2.commands:
sudo dnf install -y cmake openssl-devel git clang \
  clang-devel protobuf-compiler
rustup default stable
rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown

3.commands:
# Install WSL2 with Ubuntu, then follow Ubuntu steps
wsl --install -d Ubuntu
# Inside WSL2:
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y \
  cmake pkg-config libssl-dev git clang \
  libclang-dev protobuf-compiler

0.title: Clone and build
0.code:
git clone https://github.com/7ayLabs/7aychain.git
cd 7aychain
cargo build --release

1.title: Generate session keys
1.code:
# Start node first, then rotate keys via RPC
curl -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"id":1, "jsonrpc":"2.0", "method":"author_rotateKeys"}' \
  http://localhost:9944

0.description: Run in development mode with temporary storage

1.description: Specify chain spec (local, dev, or path to JSON)

2.description: Enable validator mode for block production

3.description: Set the data directory for the node

9.description: Allow all CORS origins for RPC

economics.label: Economics
economics.title: Staking and slashing lifecycle
economics.description: Validators must stake $7AY to participate. Honest behavior earns rewards while dishonest attestations trigger slashing.

0.description: Submit a validator registration extrinsic with your session keys. The registration enters a pending queue until the next epoch.

1.description: Once your registration is accepted by governance, your node enters the active validator set and begins participating in consensus.

2.description: Bond $7AY tokens as collateral. Higher stake increases your chance of being selected for witness circles and earning rewards.

3.description: Validators earn rewards for honest triangulation, presence attestation, and block production. Rewards are distributed per epoch.

4.description: Dishonest behavior (false attestations, downtime, equivocation) triggers slashing. A portion of your staked $7AY is burned.

witnessCircles.label: Protocol
witnessCircles.title: Witness circle participation
witnessCircles.paragraph1: When an actor declares presence, the protocol selects a subset of active validators to form a
witnessCircles.paragraph1cont: . Selection is pseudorandom, weighted by stake and constrained by network topology.
witnessCircles.paragraph2: Each validator in the circle independently measures round-trip latency to the declaring actor and to other circle members. These measurements are submitted as signed attestations during the commit phase.
witnessCircles.paragraph3: During the reveal phase, latency measurements are used for multilateration — a triangulation algorithm that estimates the actor's physical position based on network timing data from multiple reference points.
witnessCircles.paragraph4: If the position estimate from a quorum of validators converges within an acceptable threshold, the presence is finalized. Outlier measurements trigger the dispute resolution process, and validators providing false attestations face slashing.

protocolModules.description: 7aychain's runtime is composed of 16 custom pallets organized into four functional groups. Each pallet exposes extrinsics, storage, and events that developers can interact with.

0.description: Core presence declaration, epoch management, state machine, and device registration.

1.description: Position estimation, zero-knowledge proofs, and position-bound token minting.

2.description: Validator staking, dispute resolution, and autonomous anomaly detection.

3.description: Data vaults, on-chain storage, governance, trust graphs, return proofs, and group management.

updatesPage.label: Updates
updatesPage.title: Project
updatesPage.intro: This log tracks real progress across 7aychain and the 7ayLabs project — protocol milestones, shipped features, and infrastructure updates.

0.title: 7aychain v0.8.26 released
0.summary: Latest stable release with full protocol stack.
0.content: Released 7aychain v0.8.26, the latest stable version of the Layer 1 blockchain. This release includes the complete protocol stack: presence lifecycle with commit-reveal, witness-circle triangulation, ZK proof verification, validator staking and slashing, governance, vaults, semantic linking, dispute resolution, device scanning, and autonomous detection. Multi-node devnet running with 6 validators.

1.title: Governance and vault system
1.summary: On-chain governance and secure vault infrastructure shipped.
1.content: Shipped on-chain governance with proposals, voting, and delegation. Implemented the vault system with create, share, recover, register-file, request-unlock, and authorize-unlock operations. Added semantic linking for trust graphs between on-chain entities.

2.title: ZK circuits and triangulation
2.summary: Zero-knowledge proofs and multilateration algorithms integrated.
2.content: Integrated ZK proof generation and on-chain verification for privacy-preserving presence attestation. Implemented multilateration and centroid triangulation algorithms that allow validators to determine position from network latency measurements without GPS. Added position-bound tokens (PBTs) for cryptographic position claims.

3.title: Validator system and dispute resolution
3.summary: Full validator lifecycle with staking, slashing, and disputes.
3.content: Implemented the complete validator system: registration, activation, deactivation, withdrawal, staking, and slashing. Added dispute resolution with evidence submission, voting, and on-chain resolution. Validators form witness circles and participate in quorum-based presence finalization.

4.title: Multi-node devnet and CLI
4.summary: Hybrid devnet with Laud Networks CLI for protocol interaction.
4.content: Launched multi-node hybrid devnet with Alice running natively and remaining validators (Bob, Charlie, Dave, Eve, Ferdie) in Docker. Released the Laud Networks CLI with interactive commands for all protocol modules: presence, epoch, validator, triangulation, ZK, governance, vault, semantic, and more.

5.title: Presence and epoch core
5.summary: Foundational presence lifecycle and epoch management shipped.
5.content: Built the core presence state machine (Declared, Attested, Triangulated, Finalized) with epoch-bound lifecycle. Implemented the 5-state epoch model with dynamic state derivation from block timestamps. Established actor/epoch isolation and authority-controlled mutations.

6.title: Problem validation
6.summary: Validated core problem with early teams.
6.content: Validated core problem with early teams. Conducted structured interviews and research across event organizers, community builders, and logistics operators. Confirmed that presence verification is a high-pain, low-solution problem space.

description.0: We started 7ayLabs with a simple question: what if you could prove you were somewhere without revealing who you are?
description.1: That question became 7aychain — a network that verifies presence using the physics of the internet itself.

mission.label: What We're Building
mission.title: Our Mission
mission.paragraph1: 7ayLabs builds 7aychain, a new kind of blockchain. Instead of scanning your eyes or asking for ID, our network verifies that you're physically present at a location — using nothing more than your internet connection.
mission.paragraph2: Validators across the network measure connection timing to confirm your location. No GPS. No cameras. No special hardware.
mission.paragraph3: When the world can't tell real people from bots, presence becomes the most valuable signal. We're building the infrastructure to make that signal available to everyone.
mission.paragraph4: For businesses, this means fraud drops, compliance gets simpler, and every process that depends on "was this person actually here?" finally gets a reliable answer.
mission.paragraph5: For people, it means proving you were somewhere without handing over your identity. No surveillance. No data harvesting. Just proof.

vision.label: Our Long-Term View
vision.title: Our Vision
vision.paragraph1: We see a future where "being there" is a first-class digital signal — not an afterthought. As AI gets better at faking everything else, physical presence becomes the one thing that can't be simulated.
vision.paragraph2: 7ayLabs is building the infrastructure for that future. Slowly, carefully, and with the kind of rigor that real-world systems demand.

0.title: Reality over scale
0.description: We optimize for truth, not volume. If a system cannot hold up under real-world conditions, it does not belong in production.

1.title: Clarity over complexity
1.description: Every system we build is designed to answer a simple question clearly: did this actually happen?

2.title: Long-term trust
2.description: We build slowly and deliberately, validating each layer before moving forward. Infrastructure that businesses can rely on.

home.title: 7ayLabs | The Presence Layer for Web3
home.description: The first Layer 1 blockchain where physical presence is identity. 7aychain's Proof of Presence protocol triangulates human verification through network latency — no biometrics, no GPS, no hardware. Sybil-resistant by physics. Powered by $7AY.
home.ogDescription: 7aychain: a Layer 1 blockchain where validators triangulate physical presence through network latency. Sybil-resistant human verification — no GPS, no biometrics, no hardware. The presence layer for Web3.
home.twitterDescription: Your presence is your proof. 7aychain verifies humans through physics — not biometrics. The Sybil-resistant Layer 1 for Web3. $7AY

technology.title: Technology
technology.description: How 7aychain verifies human presence through physics. Explore the Proof of Presence protocol: network latency triangulation, zero-knowledge privacy, and a four-layer architecture.

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.description: Discover how Proof of Presence enables fair token drops, verified voting, secure transactions, event ticketing, verified communities, and honest reviews.

whyPresence.title: Why Presence
whyPresence.description: When bots can do everything humans can do online, the only thing left that proves you're real is being physically present. Learn how 7aychain uses physics instead of biometrics.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.description: Definitions of key 7aychain and Proof of Presence terms: witness circles, triangulation, position-bound tokens, ZK circuits, epoch lifecycle, and more.

devnet.description: Connect to the 7aychain devnet. Clone, build, and run a Substrate-based Proof of Presence node with 6 validators, 16 modules, and the full protocol stack.

about.title: About
about.description: Learn about 7ayLabs, the team building 7aychain — a Layer 1 blockchain with on-chain Proof of Presence where validators triangulate physical presence through network latency.
